title: chamaecyparis obtusa f. tetragona
intro: Chamaecyparis obtusa f. tetragona, also known as Japanese black cypress or hinoki cypress, is a cultivar of the Chamaecyparis obtusa tree native to Japan. This evergreen conifer is valued for its unique, columnar shape and fragrant wood, which is used in traditional Japanese architecture and is prized for its medicinal and therapeutic properties. The plant is rich in alpha-pinene, which has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating respiratory issues and skin conditions. Hinoki cypress is also used in herbal preparations, such as essential oils and teas, to promote relaxation and relieve stress, and is believed to offer health benefits including improved immune function and reduced anxiety.
